concurrency_note: check_architecture is a single-pass static consistency check over a declared architecture.json (and optional system-contracts.json) at a fixed content; it never inspects running code and has no concurrent/racing dimension to evade.

authority_boundary.proves: over a declared architecture.json (schema templates/formal-models/architecture-schema.json) and an optional system-contracts.json, every entry in the frozen CHECKS inventory (ADR-fh-06: dangling-endpoint, retired-node-edge, unlabelled-external, tier-inversion, label-propagation, undeclared-cross-ref, missing-tier, authored-crossing-label) fires on its textbook violation and stays silent on a clean model; proves the DECLARED model is internally consistent, NOT that code matches the model
authority_boundary.artifact: a declared C4-flavored system model file (docs/system/architecture.json) plus, when supplied via --system-contracts, the companion budget-tree/chains model (system-contracts.json)
assumptions: authority is declared-model consistency only (ADR-fh-07): the checker opens artifacts read-only and never proves the running code matches the model (that is the deferred reflexion/extraction gate)

trust_zone_crossing/region_crossing are COMPUTED-only fields; any authored non-null value is itself an authored-crossing-label finding (INV-fh-006), so a hand-authored 'safe' label cannot mask a real crossing

the retired-node-edge check exempts edges with active:false by design (a documented, retired path) — a retired-node reference on an inactive edge is a certified NON-coverage boundary, not a missed violation

cross-artifact consistency (undeclared-cross-ref) is only sampled when --system-contracts is supplied, and only over chain hop caller/callee endpoints and budget-tree telemetry_refs; absent --system-contracts it is reported not_checked, never 'passed' (CTR-fh-024)

label-propagation is declared-graph-only (no taint analysis): it compares an edge's carries[] max data-class rank against the target trust_zone ceiling and region boundary from the declared labels, and a violation carrying a valid asm_refs waiver is recorded as a documented waiver, not a finding

seeded_defect_trials.injected: architecture.json: EDG-gateway-analytics-001.to is repointed to ARC-ghost-999, an undeclared node — fires dangling-endpoint
architecture.json: ARC-analytics-001.status set to retired while the active edge EDG-gateway-analytics-001 still references it — fires retired-node-edge
architecture.json: asm_refs removed from ARC-stt-001, an external node reached by the hard edge EDG-gateway-stt-001 — fires unlabelled-external
architecture.json: EDG-gateway-analytics-001.criticality set to hard, giving tier-1 ARC-gateway-001 a hard-availability path to tier-3 ARC-analytics-001 — fires tier-inversion
architecture.json: EDG-gateway-analytics-001.carries set to [official-sensitive] into ARC-analytics-001's internal trust_zone (ceiling forbids it) with no asm_refs waiver — fires label-propagation
system-contracts.json: chains[0].hops[1].callee set to ARC-does-not-exist-999, undeclared in architecture.json (run with --system-contracts) — fires undeclared-cross-ref
architecture.json: criticality_tier removed from ARC-analytics-001 — fires missing-tier (a missing tier is a finding, not a silently-skipped node)
architecture.json: EDG-gateway-analytics-001.trust_zone_crossing authored as 'dmz->internal' (a computed-only field) — fires authored-crossing-label

architecture.json: a retired DUPLICATE of ARC-analytics-001 (status=retired) is appended to nodes[] to id-shadow the active one — a naive last-wins dict would silently collapse the duplicate and hide its retired-node-edge. The checker's pre-dict duplicate-id defense catches it (schema finding), and the retired node is not hidden (retired-node-edge also fires)

system-contracts.json: a budget-tree telemetry_ref is changed to nonexistent_binding_ms — the dangling reference is reached through the budget-tree telemetry-binding indirection layer rather than a direct chain hop or architecture.json itself; still caught as undeclared-cross-ref (run with --system-contracts)

architecture.json: ARC-analytics-001.status set to retired AND EDG-gateway-analytics-001.active set to false. The retired-node reference lives on an INACTIVE edge, which the retired-node-edge check exempts by design (a documented, retired path) — a certified NON-coverage boundary, so the gate correctly does not fire
